task_completion.description: Agent navigates the map view to the Big Island of Hawaii
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main map view shows the Big Island of Hawaii centered or clearly visible.

task_completion.description: Agent draws a path connecting Hilo (East) to Kailua-Kona (West)
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line or path is visible on the map extending from the east coast (approx 19.73°N, 155.09°W) to the west coast (approx 19.64°N, 156.00°W).
The text 'Hawaii Cross-Section' is visible in the 'Places' sidebar, the path properties dialog, or as a label on the map.

task_completion.description: Agent activates the Elevation Profile tool
task_completion.visual_evidence: An elevation profile graph is visible, typically in the bottom half of the application window, showing distance on the X-axis and elevation on the Y-axis.

task_completion.description: Elevation profile shows the peak of Mauna Kea
task_completion.visual_evidence: The graph shows a distinct mountain profile rising from near sea level at both ends to a peak in the middle. The peak elevation should be indicated around 4,200m or 13,800ft.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the screenshot to the specified path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file save dialog is visible with the filename 'hawaii_elevation_profile.png' and path '/home/ga/', or a terminal command (like scrot or gnome-screenshot) is executed with this specific path.

integrity.description: Agent uses a genuine mapping application (e.g., Google Earth Pro)
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface contains standard GIS elements like a 3D globe, layers sidebar, compass, or toolbar specific to mapping software.

integrity.description: Agent does not use a web browser to search for existing elevation profiles
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not viewing Google Images or a search engine results page for 'Hawaii elevation profile'.

integrity.description: Agent does not copy a pre-existing solution file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use terminal commands like 'cp' to copy a hidden file to the target destination without generating the content first.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Mauna Kea Peak Elevation: approx 4,207 m (13,803 ft). Start: Hilo (East). End: Kona (West). The profile should be a large single peak starting and ending near 0 elevation.
